reasoning: The historical base rate of the WHO declaring a pandemic is roughly 1 in 20 to 1 in 30 years (approx 3-5%), with the most recent ones being COVID-19 in 2020 and H1N1 in 2009. While there are ongoing threats such as H5N1 avian influenza spreading in mammal populations and the mpox PHEIC, the probability of a novel pathogen mutating, adapting for sustained human-to-human transmission, spreading globally, and receiving an official 'pandemic' declaration from the WHO within the specific 1-year window of 2025 is low. The freeze value of ~3.55% reflects this base rate and the heightened but still low risk of an emerging pandemic. I align closely with this consensus.
